item.title: Nothing against the tradition, just help me with my family event doomscrolling please!
item.description: Not even asking for a full dump, just 10, a nugget if you will. Keeping it a nice mix of tradition and memery so that neither goes unused/unnoticed and my grandmother doesn't think I'm on Tinder when scrolling through the holidays with my family.

item.title: Your fault, Imgur
item.description: Way back in 2020, I wrote a little article here on Imgur about what I thought was an interesting subject - those African-Americans who, during the War of 1812 (1812-1815), fled from American slaveowners to find their freedom with raiding British forces. Amongst those refugees, roughly 1000 men became a unique military force in British history - the "Colonial Marines" - part of the Royal Marines. This radically changed the British attacks on the American coasts of Virginia, Maryland and Georgia, and is a forgotten reason for the ability of a small British force, with no cavalry and limited artillery, to advance inland and burn Washington DC. But the story doesn't end there, as the war came to an inconclusive end, British leaders were considering creating, arming and supporting a 'black Republic' on the territory of the US (most likely Georgia) only terminated by the sudden end of the war. Colonial Marines also fought for Britain on border between Georgia and Florida - then a Spanish possession - eventually forming a unique Afro-Native American community on the Apalachicola that, after the war, was destroyed by an illegal American incursion into Spanish territory in the hope of extinguishing a 'magnet' for escaped slaves across the southern states of the United States. I thoroughly enjoyed researching this topic, and wrote many new articles on Imgur over the following years as more & more incredible facts were found - the enslaved lesbian couple from Maryland who escaped to Nova Scotia, the incredible emancipation speech of Royal Marine Colonel Nicolls ("we will unrivet the chains!"), the romantic 'private joke' of a formerly enslaved couple who joked to an American visitor about who had escaped first, the post-war settling of the Colonial Marines on Trinidad - a British colony where slavery was still practiced with concerns about a militarily-trained new community of free black people being waved aside by a dictatorial governor, the African-Americans who were offered freedom if they defended New Orleans from the British - but were afterwards returned to bondage... and so much more. My book on this incredible subject will come out in April 2024 in the UK, and June 2024 in the United States. Thank you Imgur and Imgurians, you sharpened my pen, wit, and eye, allowing me to achieve something I never anticipated. UK Amazon: https://www.amazon.co.uk/Black-Redcoats-Colonial-Marines-1814-1816/dp/1399034014 US Amazon: https://www.amazon.com/Black-Redcoats-Colonial-Marines-1814-1816/dp/1399034014 You can also pre-order the book directly from my Publishers, Pen & Sword!

i assume they mean me being tommy lee jones.. greetings online friends
man this show was so cool.. (legends of the hidden temple)
uh oh
crunchwrap obv
i hate those bags
insane
this sounds amazing, gonna rsvp now
i guess
it's a toss up
can we stop these people?
it don't make no sense!
finally
mmm hmm
sounds a bit too exciting for me
wait what
good to know?
flavor country
that's my set... have a great weekend/holidays gang!!

item.title: boggart's phone clearing dump 6
item.description:
happy friday
wherever you are inhope its a good one
or at the very least
better than yesterday
year end is almost upon us
another week, another month, another year at its end
its always interesting
getting ready to move on
on towards the future
day by day
you know what else is interesting
my lets play streams on twitch
do people call them lets plays anymore
i love the term i just dont see it anywhere much
anyways
we've been going through signalis this week
not overly scary or overly hard, good fun, spooky
finishing it up tonight at 7pm est
next week is spyro 2
cuz we just beat spyro 1 and yknow i already own it
why not go play spyro 2
if youre interested in that
find me at twitch.tv/level1cleric
where i play the games
come hang out itll be a blast
or if nothing else itll be pretty chill
either way hope your day goes well
and your weekend too. merry cromis.
but also: twitch.tv/level1cleric
item.dc:creator: boneboggart

item.title: Wrote a book! Amazon said, "No you didn't!"
item.description:
So apparently Amazon changed their policies between when I wrote my first and second books, and now posting the first-draft for free on reddit as I write them is a huge red flag. However, reddit is where I got my start. My first book was never supposed to be a book, just some writing practice/short storys. The people on /HFY were so supportive and encouraging, they practically pushed me into publishing it, and I didn't want to leave them hanging part way through! (It's a trillogy) I learned the hard way. Now if you're taking a story from reddit to amazon, you need to delete it from reddit first. I didn't want to delete it until the trillogy was complete on reddit, so instead I waited a year and a half for book 3 to be finished (and left up for a few months so all the late comers had a chance to read it) before being taken down.
Okay. The contract thing actually made me laugh once I found somone who explained it to me. (Like a dozen automated responses, several emails, and a few live chats latter!) Apparently it wouldn't have been an issue if I'd deleted it from reddit before trying to publish it the first time. Learn from my mistakes! If you're gonna publish stuff you've posted elsewhere, delete it first!
I'm still gonna post the first drafts of all my stuff on reddit as I'm writing it. The people there helped me develop my passion for writing, and I'm not gonna leave them hanging! I'm just gonna delete my stories (after they've been up a few months) before publishing from now on. If you wanna read some of my stuff, look me up on the HFY subbreddit, or just keep an eye out here, as I cross-post a lot of my short stories here as well. And yes, for those wondering, I am in the middle of editing book 3 for publication as I post this. Already started writing and posting a new series too, "Of Men and Spiders," which can still be found on reddit. (Yeah, I'm really original with naming!)
Holy shit! Is that a big deal? I feel like that's a big deal! How the hell did I get a #1 New Release on amazon? I hadn't sold that many coppies last I checked, so I'm not sure what their metric is. Apparently it's updated hourly, so it can be a "here today gone tomarrow" kinda thing, but still, I'm kinda giddy! Here's a link for anyone interested: https://www.amazon.com/Men-Dragons-New-Foundations-ebook/dp/B0CQ7FQ1ZJ?ref_=ast_author_mpb
My co-author, Alfred, doing the loud purrs. He has a cat bed on the computer table right next to me, where he's spent hours chilling as I write.
item.dc:creator: DrBlackJack

item.title: There is a a recording in which Donald Trump appears to urge two canvassers in Wayne County, Michigan, to not certify the 2020 presidential election.
item.description: Joe Gallina, an affiliate of pro-Democratic campaign group 'Call to Activism,' said "[T]here is nothing, and as an attorney I'm telling you, there is nothing more persuasive than a defendant himself being recorded committing a crime. There is nothing more persuasive in the eyes of the law. https://www.newsweek.com/donald-trump-michigan-tape-perfect-evidenceattorney-1854829
item.dc:creator: MenloPart
